Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Zara Okoye damage incident communications using search_emails (query: "Okoye" or "prototype damage" or "Tamara Byrd" or similar). Find: (a) Tamara Byrd's formal damage complaint to Emeka Diallo on April 15, requesting a complete incident report within 48 hours; (b) Emeka's initial acknowledgment promising the report, CC'ing chloe.vance@moveops.com; (c) Emeka's complete incident report sent to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com, CC rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com, covering chain of custody timeline, process failure analysis (Blessing's remote packing checklist sign-off, no MoveOps physical inspection), damage assessment (cracked servo motor housing, joint calibration misalignment on Unit #1), and remedial actions; (d) Zara Okoye's damage report to Tamara describing the damage to Unit #1. Discovery: Emeka sent a comprehensive report that acknowledges MoveOps process failures including the remote sign-off.
OE 2: Search Slack for Okoye damage incident discussion using conversations_search_messages (query: "Okoye" or "prototype" or "damage" or "Heartland" or similar). Find in #operations (C006): (a) Emeka's HIGH-SEVERITY CLIENT ESCALATION post flagging the $90,000 prototype damage; (b) Chloe's timeline investigation with packing specs from work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE; (c) Chloe's discovery that the delivery receipt at the Atlanta address was signed by Kyle Britten (a Mosaic Robotics intern who was at the apartment) instead of Zara Okoye, who was at new employee orientation; (d) Marcus Thorne's financial analysis identifying the $40,000 insurance coverage gap (vendor liability caps at $50,000 per item vs $90,000 prototype value); (e) Chloe's finalized incident summary consolidating all findings for Emeka to draft the formal report.
OE 3: Search CRM for Mosaic Robotics damage incident engagement using crm_search_companies (query: "Mosaic") or crm_search_deals (query: "Mosaic" or "Okoye"). Find: (a) engagement_mosaic_damage_incident_041526 -- Emeka's detailed damage incident log with full chain of custody timeline, noting Blessing Okafor's remote packing sign-off at ~9:45 AM from Philadelphia while coordinating Tyler Okonkwo, and Kyle Britten signing the delivery receipt at 9:15 AM on April 15; (b) deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk -- Zara Okoye relocation deal ($2,700) flagged with RISK: DAMAGE CLAIM, noting the $40,000 insurance gap and pending claim adjudication.
OE 4: Search Airtable for Zara Okoye and Keiko Tanaka relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblRelocations01, query: "Zara Okoye" or "Keiko Tanaka" or similar). Find: (a) recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta -- Name: Zara Okoye, Company: Mosaic Robotics, Status: Completed, Origin: Savannah GA, Destination: Atlanta GA, Move Start: 2026-04-14, Move End: 2026-04-16, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor, Special Requirements listing prototype crating specs; (b) recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle -- Name: Keiko Tanaka, Company: Mosaic Robotics, Status: In Progress, Origin: Columbus OH, Destination: Seattle WA, Move Start: 2026-04-15, Move End: 2026-04-23, Coordinator: Javier Morales, Special Requirements listing the Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter with Class 3B CO2 laser tube.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks for insurance claim and damage-related entries using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria related to "Mosaic" or "damage") and/or quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to "Mosaic"). Find: (a) bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 -- $90,000 pending liability accrual for damaged Unit #1, noting vendor liability coverage caps at $50,000 per item and $40,000 represents MoveOps direct exposure if Heartland does not accept fault, marked "DO NOT PAY -- accrual entry only, pending insurance resolution"; (b) credit memo cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001 -- negative $90,000 credit for insurance claim pending adjudication, noting $50,000 vendor coverage and $40,000 remaining exposure; (c) bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 -- $50,000 DOT/PHMSA contingent penalty accrual for potential hazmat compliance violations per Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, specifically referencing the missing Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate documentation gap.
OE 6: Search emails for Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate status using search_emails (query: "Keiko Tanaka" or "placarding" or "DOT" or "Swift" or similar). Find: (a) Chloe's email to Greg Pallone (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) requesting the signed placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment (Columbus to Seattle); (b) Greg Pallone's response confirming that DOT hazmat placarding was completed by driver Mike Becerra at the Columbus pickup location on April 15, and providing a scanned PDF of the signed certificate (Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538); (c) Marcus Thorne's formal response to DOT (Linda Castellano at PHMSA) acknowledging the Tanaka documentation gap and requesting a deadline extension. Discovery: Swift DID complete the placarding and sent a scanned certificate, but MoveOps needs to verify it is a signed original or certified copy per DOT requirements.
OE 7: Search Linear for Keiko Tanaka DOT compliance tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Tanaka" or "DOT" or "placarding" or similar). Find: (a) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (#2) -- "DOT Docs -- Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser", state: in_progress, priority: 1, assigned to Chloe Vance, due: 2026-04-23; (b) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (#5) -- "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations -- URGENT", state: todo, priority: 1, assigned to Chloe Vance, due: 2026-04-23. Discovery: The subtask is still in "todo" state even though Swift has already sent the scanned certificate.
OE 8: Search Linear for any existing Okoye damage tracking ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Okoye" or "damage" or "prototype" or similar). Discovery: No Linear ticket exists specifically for tracking the Okoye prototype damage incident resolution, despite the incident being logged in CRM and discussed extensively in Slack and email.
OE 9: Look up Emeka Diallo's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Emeka Diallo" or "Emeka"). Find emeka.diallo@moveops.com.
OE 10: Create or update Linear tickets to address gaps found in the investigation using linear_create_issue and/or linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment: (a) Create a new Linear ticket for tracking the Okoye prototype damage incident resolution (title related to "Zara Okoye Prototype Damage" or similar, description covering the $90,000 Unit #1 damage, $40,000 insurance gap, delivery receipt issue, pending claim adjudication); and/or (b) Update the Keiko Tanaka DOT subtask (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert #5) or add a comment to the parent ticket (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic #2) reflecting that Swift has confirmed placarding was completed and sent a scanned PDF. This fulfills the prompt's "If any records or tickets are wrong or incomplete, fix them."
OE 11: Post a status update in #operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006). The message should provide Chloe with a consolidated update covering: (a) Okoye move timeline summary; (b) what Emeka sent Tamara in the incident report and whether it aligns with internal records; (c) insurance claim status and the $40,000 coverage gap; (d) the delivery receipt issue (Kyle Britten signed instead of Zara); (e) Keiko placarding certificate status (Swift confirmed, scanned PDF received); (f) any record or ticket corrections made. This fulfills the prompt's "Post a status update in #operations so Chloe has everything in one place."
OE 12: Send a message to Emeka Diallo using send_email (sender: blessing.okafor@moveops.com, recipients: emeka.diallo@moveops.com) or a Slack message, looping him in on relevant findings from the investigation. The message should include findings Emeka should know about such as the delivery receipt issue, insurance gap confirmation, packing checklist concerns, and/or Keiko placarding status. This fulfills the prompt's "loop Emeka in on anything he should know about."

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
